docs.title: Lite-Anvil - Code Editor Built in Rust
Lite-Anvil
docs.text:
A fast code editor built in Rust with SDL3.

Get Started View on GitHub

docs.title: Built-in LSP
docs.text: Diagnostics, completion, hover, go-to-definition, references, inlay hints, and more for Rust, Python, TypeScript, Go, C/C++, and other languages.

docs.title: Embedded Terminal
docs.text: Full PTY terminal with ANSI/VT100 colors, scrollback, and multi-terminal support.

docs.title: Git Integration
docs.text: Git status view, gutter markers, per-line blame annotations, file commit log, push, pull, commit, stash from the command palette.

docs.title: 50+ Syntax Grammars
docs.text: Rust, Go, Python, TypeScript, C/C++, Java, Kotlin, Scala, F#, C#, Haskell, Zig, Elixir, Erlang, OCaml, Gleam, and many more.

docs.title: Fast
docs.text: Pure Rust. Sub-second startup. Low memory footprint. Ring-buffer terminal scrollback, glyph cache with ASCII pre-warming, merged undo entries.

docs.title: Multi-Cursor Editing
docs.text: Ctrl+Shift+Up/Down to add cursors. Typing, deletion, and movement apply to all cursors simultaneously.

docs.title: Code Folding & Minimap
docs.text: Fold code blocks with Ctrl+Shift+[. Syntax-colored minimap with click-to-scroll.

docs.title: Session Restore
docs.text: Open files, active tab, font scale, and project root persist across restarts. Recent projects list for quick switching.

docs.title: Bookmarks & Find-in-Selection
docs.text: Ctrl+F4 to bookmark lines, F4/Shift+F4 to navigate. Find scopes to the selection when a multi-line region is active.

docs.title: Bracket Pair Colorization
docs.text: Matching brackets colored by nesting depth (gold, pink, blue) like VS Code.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
Lite-Anvil is a fork of Lite XL, rewritten from the ground up in Rust.

Languages 50+ syntax grammars, built-in LSP configurations Platform Linux, macOS, Windows License MIT Rust version 1.85+

docs.title: About
docs.text: Lite-Anvil is a code editor forked from Lite XL, rewritten from the ground up in Rust with SDL3.

docs.title: Origins
docs.text: Lite XL is a C-based lightweight editor inspired by lite. Lite-Anvil replaced the entire codebase with Rust -- rendering, editing, LSP, terminal, and all UI.

docs.title: License
docs.text:
MIT -- see LICENSE.

Font: Lilex

Documentation: Built with Material for MkDocs by Martin Donath.

docs.title: Author
docs.text: Dan Pozmanter

docs.title: General
docs.text: Key Action Ctrl+P Command palette Ctrl+O Open file (supports file:42 to go to line) Ctrl+Shift+O Open file from project Ctrl+Shift+R Open recent file / project Ctrl+N New file Ctrl+S Save (save-as for unnamed files) Ctrl+Shift+S Save as Ctrl+W Close tab Ctrl+Q Quit Ctrl+= / Ctrl+- Increase / decrease font size Ctrl+0 Reset font size Ctrl+B Toggle sidebar Ctrl+M Toggle minimap Alt+Z Toggle line wrapping Ctrl+Shift+H Toggle whitespace rendering Ctrl+Shift+P Cycle theme F11 Toggle fullscreen Ctrl+`` / F5 Toggle terminal Ctrl+Shift+T New terminal

docs.title: Editing
docs.text: Key Action Ctrl+D Select word / add next occurrence Ctrl+L Select line Ctrl+A Select all Ctrl+/ Toggle line comment (language-aware) Ctrl+Up / Ctrl+Down Move line up / down Ctrl+Shift+D Duplicate line Ctrl+Shift+K Delete line Ctrl+J Join lines Ctrl+Shift+Up/Down Add cursor above / below Ctrl+Shift+[ Fold code block Ctrl+Shift+] Unfold code block Ctrl+Shift+\ Unfold all

docs.title: Find & Replace
docs.text: Key Action Ctrl+F Find in file Alt+F Replace in file F3 / Enter Next match Shift+F3 / Shift+Enter Previous match Alt+R Toggle regex (inside find bar) Alt+W Toggle whole word (inside find bar) Alt+I Toggle case-insensitive (inside find bar) Alt+S Toggle find-in-selection (inside find bar) Ctrl+Enter Replace current match and find next Ctrl+Shift+F Find in files (project search) Alt+Shift+F Replace in files (project replace)

docs.title: LSP
docs.text: Key Action F12 Go to definition Ctrl+F12 Go to implementation Shift+F12 Find references Ctrl+Shift+F12 Go to type definition Ctrl+K Hover

docs.title: Bookmarks
docs.text:
Key Action Ctrl+F4 Toggle bookmark on current line F4 Jump to next bookmark Shift+F4 Jump to previous bookmark

Bookmarked lines show an accent-colored marker in the gutter. Bookmarks wrap around and are per-document.

docs.title: Navigation
docs.text: Key Action Ctrl+G Go to line Ctrl+Tab / Ctrl+Shift+Tab Next / previous tab Ctrl+PageUp / Ctrl+PageDown Move tab left / right Ctrl+Shift+G Git status

docs.title: Command Palette
docs.text:
Press Ctrl+P to open the command palette. All commands are searchable. The palette filters out raw key-input commands and only shows meaningful actions. Git commands are prefixed with "Git" (e.g. Git Pull, Git Push, Git Commit, Git Stash).

Additional commands available from the palette:

  • Sort Lines, Upper Case, Lower Case
  • Open User Settings
  • Toggle Minimap, Toggle Line Wrapping, Toggle Whitespace
  • Toggle Hidden Files -- show/hide dotfiles in the sidebar
  • Check For Updates -- query GitHub for a newer release
  • Git Pull, Git Push, Git Commit, Git Stash, Git Status
  • Git Blame -- toggle per-line blame annotations (author + date)
  • Git Log -- show the last 50 commits for the active file
  • Close All, Close All Others

docs.title: Sidebar
docs.text: File icons in the sidebar are color-coded by extension (e.g. Rust files in red, Python in blue, Go in cyan). Colors are loaded from data/assets/file_icons.json. Hidden files (dotfiles) are hidden by default; toggle visibility with Toggle Hidden Files from the command palette.

docs.title: Sidebar Context Menu
docs.text:
Right-click a file or folder in the sidebar:

  • Open -- open the file
  • Copy Path -- copy absolute path to clipboard
  • Copy Relative Path -- copy project-relative path
  • Refresh -- rescan the project tree
  • Rename / Delete -- file operations
  • New File / New Folder -- create in the selected directory

docs.title: LSP Support
docs.text: Lite-Anvil includes built-in configurations for the following language servers. Install the binary and it works automatically -- no configuration needed.

docs.title: Recommended Language Servers
docs.text: Language Server Install Rust rust-analyzer rustup component add rust-analyzer Python pyright-langserver pip install pyright or npm install -g pyright Go gopls go install golang.org/x/tools/gopls@latest JavaScript / TypeScript / TSX typescript-language-server npm install -g typescript-language-server typescript C / C++ clangd Package manager (e.g. apt install clangd) Java jdtls Eclipse JDT.LS Kotlin kotlin-language-server GitHub releases C# OmniSharp dotnet tool install -g OmniSharp F# fsautocomplete dotnet tool install -g fsautocomplete Scala metals Metals PHP intelephense npm install -g intelephense Ruby ruby-lsp gem install ruby-lsp Lua lua-language-server GitHub releases Bash bash-language-server npm install -g bash-language-server Zig zls ZLS Haskell haskell-language-server ghcup install hls Elixir elixir-ls ElixirLS Erlang erlang_ls erlang_ls OCaml ocamllsp opam install ocaml-lsp-server Gleam gleam lsp Bundled with gleam CLI Dart dart language-server Bundled with Dart SDK Swift sourcekit-lsp Bundled with Swift toolchain Julia LanguageServer.jl julia -e 'using Pkg; Pkg.add("LanguageServer")' Clojure clojure-lsp GitHub releases Crystal crystalline Crystalline

docs.title: Custom LSP Configuration
docs.text:
Create lsp.json in your user config directory or project root to add servers or override builtins:

{
  "my_server": {
    "command": ["my-lsp", "--stdio"],
    "filetypes": ["mylang"],
    "rootPatterns": ["myproject.toml", ".git"]
  }
}

Set "autostart": false to disable a builtin server.

docs.title: Syntax Highlighting
docs.text:
51 built-in syntax grammars:

Category Languages Systems Assembly, C, C++, D, Rust, Zig JVM Java, Kotlin, Scala, Groovy, Clojure Web JavaScript, TypeScript, TSX, HTML, CSS, Vue, Svelte, PHP .NET C#, F# Scripting Python, Ruby, Lua, Bash, PowerShell, R, Lisp Functional Haskell, OCaml, Elixir, Erlang, Gleam, Julia, Crystal Other Go, Dart, Swift Data/Config JSON (via JS), TOML, YAML, INI, XML, CSV, Env, Fstab, SQL, PostgreSQL, Meson Markup Markdown, Dockerfile

XML highlighting also covers .csproj, .fsproj, .vbproj, .vcxproj, .sln, .props, .targets, .nuspec, .pom, .svg, .plist, .xaml.

Groovy highlighting covers .gradle files. Kotlin highlighting covers .gradle.kts files (via .kts extension).

Toggle line comments (Ctrl+/) automatically picks the correct marker for the active language -- // for Rust/C/JS, # for Python/Bash/TOML, -- for Lua/SQL/Haskell, ; for Assembly/Lisp/INI, % for Erlang, and block-comment wrapping (<!-- --> for HTML/Markdown/XML/Vue, /* */ for CSS, (* *) for OCaml) for languages without a line-comment form.

docs.title: Test Runner
docs.text:
The test runner auto-detects your project's framework and runs tests in a terminal pane.

Language Detection Tool Run All Run File Rust Cargo.toml cargo cargo test cargo test <module> JavaScript / TypeScript package.json vitest, jest, or npm npx vitest run / npx jest / npm test <runner> <file> Python pytest.ini, conftest.py, pyproject.toml pytest or unittest python -m pytest -v pytest <file> Go go.mod go test go test ./... go test -v ./<dir> C# / F# .sln, .csproj, .fsproj dotnet test dotnet test dotnet test --filter <class> Java / Kotlin / Scala build.gradle[.kts] Gradle ./gradlew test ./gradlew test --tests "*Class*" Java / Kotlin / Scala pom.xml Maven mvn test mvn test -Dtest="Class" Scala build.sbt sbt sbt test sbt "testOnly *Class*" PHP phpunit.xml[.dist] PHPUnit ./vendor/bin/phpunit phpunit <file> Any Makefile make make test --

docs.title: Configuration
docs.text: Lite-Anvil is configured via a TOML file. Open it from the sidebar settings icon or via the command palette: Open User Settings.

docs.title: Config location
docs.text: Platform Path Linux ~/.config/lite-anvil/config.toml macOS ~/Library/Application Support/lite-anvil/config.toml Windows %APPDATA%\lite-anvil\config.toml

docs.title: Key options
docs.text:
theme = "dark_default"         # dark_default, light_default, fall, summer, textadept
indent_size = 2
tab_type = "soft"              # "soft" (spaces) or "hard" (tabs)
line_endings = "lf"            # "lf" or "crlf"
line_limit = 80
line_height = 1.2
highlight_current_line = true
draw_whitespace = false
borderless = false
max_tabs = 8
blink_period = 0.8
disable_blink = false
mac_command_as_ctrl = true     # macOS: fold Cmd into Ctrl (on by default on Mac)
format_on_paste = true         # convert pasted indent to match document style

[lsp]
load_on_startup = true
semantic_highlighting = true
inline_diagnostics = true
format_on_save = true

[terminal]
placement = "bottom"
reuse_mode = "pane"

[ui]
padding_x = 14
padding_y = 7
caret_width = 2
scrollbar_size = 4
tab_width = 170

[fonts.ui]
path = "/path/to/your/font.ttf"
size = 15

[fonts.code]
path = "/path/to/your/mono.ttf"
size = 15

Custom keybindings can be added under [keybindings]:

[keybindings]
"ctrl+shift+l" = "doc:select-lines"
"alt+shift+f" = "lsp:format-document"

docs.title: Command line options
docs.text:
lite-anvil [file...]          Open files
lite-anvil file.rs:42         Open file at line 42
lite-anvil -v                 Verbose mode (log LSP errors to stderr)
lite-anvil --verbose          Same as -v

The :N line-number suffix also works in the file picker (Ctrl+O).

docs.title: Themes
docs.text: Cycle themes with Ctrl+Shift+P or the command palette. JSON theme files are in data/assets/themes/.

docs.title: Prebuilt Binaries
docs.text: Download the latest release for your platform from GitHub Releases.

docs.title: Linux
docs.text:
Extract the archive, copy the binary and data directory:

cp lite-anvil ~/.local/bin/
cp -r data ~/.local/share/lite-anvil/

Optional -- register for "Open With" on supported file types:

cp resources/linux/com.lite_anvil.LiteAnvil.desktop ~/.local/share/applications/
cp resources/icons/lite-anvil.png ~/.local/share/icons/hicolor/128x128/apps/
update-desktop-database ~/.local/share/applications/

docs.title: macOS
docs.text:
1. Download the .app bundle or build one (see below).
  2. Move LiteAnvil.app to /Applications.
  3. Sign the bundle so macOS doesn't block it:
codesign --force --deep --sign - --timestamp=none /Applications/LiteAnvil.app

If macOS still refuses to open it (Gatekeeper quarantine), remove the quarantine attribute:

sudo xattr -dr com.apple.quarantine /Applications/LiteAnvil.app

docs.title: Windows
docs.text:
Extract the archive. Copy lite-anvil.exe and the data/ directory wherever you like.

Optional -- register file associations:

powershell -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File resources\windows\install-file-associations.ps1

docs.title: Requirements
docs.text:
• Rust 1.85+ via rustup
  • System libraries:
Library Ubuntu/Debian Fedora Arch macOS (Homebrew) SDL3 libsdl3-dev SDL3-devel sdl3 sdl3 FreeType2 libfreetype6-dev freetype-devel freetype2 freetype PCRE2 libpcre2-dev pcre2-devel pcre2 pcre2

docs.title: Build & Run
docs.text:
git clone https://github.com/danpozmanter/lite-anvil.git
cd lite-anvil
cargo build --release
./target/release/lite-anvil [path]

docs.title: macOS App Bundle (from source)
docs.text:
mkdir -p LiteAnvil.app/Contents/MacOS
cp target/release/lite-anvil LiteAnvil.app/Contents/MacOS/
cp -r data LiteAnvil.app/Contents/MacOS/
cp resources/macos/Info.plist LiteAnvil.app/Contents/
codesign --force --deep --sign - --timestamp=none LiteAnvil.app

docs.title: Debian Package
docs.text:
cargo install cargo-deb
cargo deb --no-build

docs.title: Configuration
docs.text:
User config location:

Platform Path Linux ~/.config/lite-anvil/ macOS ~/Library/Application Support/lite-anvil/ Windows %APPDATA%\lite-anvil\

Key files:

  • config.toml -- editor settings (see Configuration)
